title: The inline-asm contract with the compiler
description: Inline asm is opaque to the optimizer, so its operand constraints, clobbers, and volatility are the only information the compiler has about its effects.
body: Use when reasoning about why inline asm misbehaves at high optimization. The compiler cannot see inside an asm block; it relies entirely on the declared input/output constraints, the clobber list (registers, 'cc' flags, 'memory'), and the volatile marker to schedule code around it. If the declaration understates what the block does, the optimizer may reuse a clobbered register, drop a side-effecting block, or reorder it. Do not treat the constraint list as documentation — it is a binding contract. Actions: declare every effect truthfully and test at -O2/-O3. Validation signals include identical results across optimization levels matching a reference. Risk boundary: an understated clobber is a latent miscompile that surfaces only when the optimizer happens to reuse the register; treat the declaration as correctness-critical.
